shell.readOnly: Read-only, you don't have permission to save changes here.
shell.unsaved: Unsaved changes. Press Save to apply.

faqs.title: FAQ entries
faqs.description: Public question/answer entries, each with a shareable link at <1>/faq/<slug></1> and listed at <3>/faqs</3>. Visible to anyone, signed in or not. Answers accept Markdown or HTML.
faqs.deleteConfirm: Delete the FAQ "{{question}}"? The /faq/{{slug}} link will 404.

earning.resetDefaultsConfirm: Reset Awards to defaults? Any unsaved changes will be discarded.
earning.awardsDescription: Every numeric value the XP / Currency engine reads. Changes take effect on the next earn. Body floor and whisper amounts apply per message; presence amounts are per 5-minute block.
earning.enabledHelp: Master kill-switch for the entire XP / Currency system. When off, no source awards anything.
earning.sourceMatrixDescription: Toggle XP and Currency awards independently for each source. Either flag off → that pool earns zero from that source even if the amount below is non-zero.
earning.bodyFloorHelp: Messages shorter than this earn nothing.
earning.presenceBlockHelp: Length of one presence-award block. Takes effect on next server boot.
earning.presenceCapHelp: Max blocks per pool per day. Past the cap, presence still records activity but earns 0.
earning.divisorDescription: Multiplier applied to per-character IC awards when the user has more than one logged-in character. 1.0 = each character earns the full configured rate (current spec). Lower throttles multi-character earning.
earning.lengthBonusDescription: Reward longer effortful posts with a multiplier on the per-kind XP+Currency. Linear interpolation from 1.0× at Floor up to Max× at Ceil; above Ceil clamps to Max. Disable per-kind to keep that source on the flat base rate.
earning.floorCharsHelp: At or below this length: 1.0× (base rate).
earning.ceilCharsHelp: At or above this length: Max multiplier (clamped).
earning.maxMultiplierHelp: Cap on the multiplier at or above Ceil. 1.0–10.0.
earning.spamDescription: Heuristics that drop the award to zero on suspect messages. Each check has its own threshold; setting any to 0 disables that check individually. Master switch (Enabled) bypasses every check at once. Flagged messages still post normally, only the award is denied, and the ledger metadata records why so admins can audit and tune.
earning.minLengthHelp: Skip every heuristic below this length, short messages are not flagged.
earning.uniqueCharHelp: Flag if unique-chars / total-chars is below this. 0.18 catches "aaaaaaaaaa" and "!!!!!!!". 0 disables.
earning.dominantTokenHelp: Flag if any single word repeats > this share of total tokens. 0.55 catches "spam spam spam spam". 0 disables.
earning.echoLookbackHelp: How many recent messages-per-user to compare for exact-duplicate detection. 0 disables.
earning.transfersDescription: Anti-abuse gates on user-to-user Currency transfers.
earning.backfillDescription: At first boot the engine credited XP per pre-existing message at the rate below. `completedAt` is set automatically by the backfill job; editing it is not exposed.
earning.backfillNotRun: Not yet run, will fire on next boot.
earning.ranksDescription: Edit thresholds, asset URLs, and enable flags. Saved changes take effect immediately; threshold edits re-place every earning row automatically. Disabling a rank is a soft close , existing rank-holders keep their rank, but new earners skip past it.
earning.keySlugTitle: Lowercase letters, digits, underscores. Must start with a letter.
earning.defaultTiersNote: Four default tiers (I, II, III, IV: Verified) are created automatically with zero thresholds. Edit them below after creating.
earning.rankInUse: Cannot delete: {{users}} users + {{characters}} characters currently hold this rank. Disable it instead.
earning.disableInsteadTitle: Disable instead, users currently hold this rank.
earning.stylesDescription: HTML + CSS templates with a <1>{username}</1> placeholder. Edits go live on the next /earning/me fetch (every user sees the new CSS without a reload). Built-in seed styles can be rewritten but not deleted.
earning.selectStyleHint: Select a style from the list or create a new one.
earning.deleteStyleConfirm: Delete "{{name}}"? Users who own it will lose access.
earning.templateHelp: <0>{username}</0> drops the name as one text run. <2>{username-span}</2> wraps each character in its own<4><span data-i="N"></4> so per-character CSS works, target via <6>span[data-i="0"]</6> or<8>:nth-child()</8> for alternating colors, per-letter animations, etc.
earning.builtinDeleteError: Built-in borders cannot be deleted; disable instead
earning.deleteBorderConfirmInUse: Delete "{{name}}"? {{owners}} owner(s), {{equipped}} equip(s) will be cleared.
earning.rarityPlaceholder: common / rare / epic / legendary / mythic / exotic / atmospheric
earning.imageUrlLabel: Image URL (PNG / APNG / WebP, transparent center). Leave blank if using a template.
earning.borderTemplateLabel: Template (HTML, uses literal `{avatar}` for the avatar slot). Leave blank if using an image URL.
earning.borderCssLabel: Style CSS (scoped to the `.b-<key>` chain referenced by the template)
earning.flairDescription: Edit the buyable cosmetics catalog. Rank borders are priced per-rank in the Ranks tab; this surface covers everything else.
earning.moderationDescription: Wipe a user's free-form Flair content (banner URL, typing phrase) without touching their ownership of the cosmetic.
earning.clearBannerHint: Empties the banner-URL slot for the target identity. Use after a hotlinked image report.
earning.clearTypingHint: Empties the custom typing phrase for the target identity. Use after a harassment / language report.
earning.clearRoomPresenceHint: Wipes both the join and leave room broadcast templates for the target identity. Use after an abusive room-presence flair report.
earning.clearSessionHint: Wipes both the login and logout broadcast templates. Master-only; character id is ignored.
earning.characterIdLabel: Character id (optional, leave blank to clear master/OOC)
earning.rankBorderNote: The cost field for <1>rank_border</1> is ignored, actual per-rank prices live on each rank's Tier IV row in the Ranks tab.
earning.itemsDescription: Catalog of collectible items users buy with Currency, hand to each other via /give, or toss at each other via /throw and /drop. Every identity (OOC master + each character) keeps an independent inventory; nothing is shared across identities.
earning.itemSearchPlaceholder: Search by name, key, or alias…
earning.noItemMatches: No items match the current filter.
earning.builtinBadgeTitle: Seeded built-in. Delete-protected; every field still editable.
earning.deleteItemConfirmOwners_one: Delete "{{name}}"? {{count}} identity owns it, every stack will be wiped.
earning.deleteItemConfirmOwners_other: Delete "{{name}}"? {{count}} identities own it, every stack will be wiped.
earning.availNotInShop: Exists, but not in the shop right now.
earning.availSince: On sale since {{time}}. No end date.
earning.aliasesHelp: Casual names users can type instead of the canonical name. Whitespace + case ignored, duplicates dropped.
earning.stackLimitTitle: Vestigial. Players accumulate without ceiling, /give, /raffle, and the Shop ignore this value. Kept for future re-introduction.
earning.categoryHelp: Drives the shop bucket. <1>pet</1> routes to the 5-slot Pet Collection; everything else to the 10-slot Item Collection.
earning.commandMessagesLegend: Command messages, one template per line. Placeholders: {sender} {target} {num} {item_name} {icon} (inline image of this item's icon). Leave blank to disable that command for this item.
earning.giveHint: Transfers the item to the target's inventory.
earning.throwHint: Consumes the item as a silly weapon, target gets nothing.
earning.dropHint: Consumes the item as a 'drop on someone' gag, target gets nothing.
earning.testGrantsDescription: Quick path to preview ranks, borders, and styles in chat / userlist / profile without grinding XP. Every grant goes through the live engine (ledger + socket events), so the recipient's dashboard updates immediately. Owner only.
earning.grantCurrencyHelp: Positive amount credits, negative debits. Goes to master pool.
earning.grantXpHelp: Recomputes rank/tier via the resolver after the credit. Negative XP doesn't decrease peak rank.
earning.setRankDescription: Direct rank-tier override. Bumps XP to the tier's threshold so the next earn doesn't drop the user back. Also bumps the eligibility peak so all lower-rank borders unlock for purchase.
earning.grantBorderDescription: Inserts ownership for the chosen rank's border. Bypasses the normal Tier IV eligibility gate, handy for previewing every frame without climbing.
earning.grantStyleDescription: Inserts ownership for the chosen style without a Currency charge. Recipient still needs to equip it from the dashboard.
earning.grantItemDescription: Deposit (positive) or revoke (negative) units of an item into the target's OOC master inventory. Bypasses the shop's enabled / forSale / sale-window checks so admins can pre-seed testers with items that aren't yet on sale. Character-scoped grants live on the character's own inventory; this row only writes the OOC pool. Stack-cap enforced server-side; overflow returns a 409.
earning.grantFreeformDescription: Inserts ownership for the chosen free-form border (master pool). Idempotent. Auto-equips on first acquisition if the identity has no freeform border equipped, matches the user-facing purchase behavior.
earning.revokeFreeformDescription: Removes ownership of the chosen free-form border from the target's master pool. If they had it equipped, the equip slot clears too. Idempotent on unowned.
earning.revokeRankBorderDescription: Removes the chosen rank-tier border from the target's owned set. Clears the equip slot if it pointed at this border.
earning.revokeStyleDescription: Removes the chosen name-style ownership row. Clears the active-cosmetics slot if the style was equipped.
earning.noItemsInCatalog: No items in the catalog yet, create one in the Items sub-tab first.
earning.flashSaleDescription: One row per category goes on sale each UTC day. Today's picks are read-only, the resolver has already chosen. Queue a specific row for any future date, or leave a date un-queued to keep it random. Override targets that get disabled in the catalog before resolution day still get picked (admin intent wins over availability).
earning.queueDescription: Pick a row + an optional per-pick discount. Click Save to queue. Setting target to "(random)" removes any existing queue for that slot.
earning.catalogBackupDescription: Per-catalog ZIP export + import. Imports are <1>upsert by key</1>, rows you didn't include keep their existing values. Bundled `/uploads/*` images extract back to their original paths so a round-trip restores custom art too. Re-importing a file you just exported is a clean no-op.
earning.transferBordersHelp: Border image + cost on each Tier IV. Surgical: only border columns are touched on import.
earning.transferFreeformHelp: Non-rank-tied border catalog (image-mode and template+CSS-mode). Ownership ledgers are NOT exported.
earning.transferRanksHelp: Full rank hierarchy: ranks + tiers + sigils + borders.
earning.importConfirm: Import {{file}} into {{catalog}}? This UPSERTS by key, existing rows with matching keys will be overwritten.
earning.importDone: Import done. Inserted {{inserted}}, updated {{updated}}, {{assets}} asset(s) written.

permissions.title: Roles & Permissions
permissions.readOnlyNote: Read-only, you can view the matrix but not edit it. Editing requires the<1>manage_permissions</1>key.
permissions.footnote: Owner row is locked-on by definition, the bypass is hardcoded in the server. Toggling any cell takes effect immediately; affected users see the new permissions on their next /auth/me poll (within 60 seconds, no re-login). Click the <1>log</1> button next to a permission key to jump to its grant history.
permissions.privacyTitle: Privacy-sensitive, granting this key reveals other users' private content.
permissions.pickUserHint: Pick a user above to edit their overrides.
permissions.userHistoryTitle: Show grant history for this user
permissions.selfNote: You cannot edit your own overrides. Use a different owner account.
permissions.cycleFromRoleGrant: Currently inheriting GRANT from role. Click → explicitly grant.
permissions.cycleFromRoleDeny: Currently inheriting DENY from role. Click → explicitly grant.
permissions.cycleRevoked: Explicitly revoked. Click → clear (fall back to role).
permissions.integrityDescription: Runs every safety invariant (resolver precedence, owner-only key leaks, orphan catalog keys, fallback engagement) against the live cache.
permissions.sensitiveDescription: Informational. Shows what each role and user currently holds across the privacy-sensitive and high-impact key sets, so you can confirm the grants still match your intent.
permissions.ownerNote: <0>Owner</0> holds every permission by bypass, including all keys listed below, and is not enumerated here.
permissions.noHolders: No role or user currently holds any sensitive key. Only owners have them.
permissions.byUserOverridesHelp: Grants applied to a specific account on top of (or independent of) their role. A user with role-level holdings also appears in the By role section above.

users.deleteConfirm:
DELETE user "{{name}}"?

This cascades through their characters, room memberships, sessions, and bans. Their messages keep the snapshotted display name in history but their account is gone permanently. This cannot be undone.
users.description: Every registered account, including disabled ones. Search matches username, email, and character name (so a persona name finds its owning OOC account). Editing role to "admin" grants global moderation - same as <1>/promoteadmin</1>. "masteradmin" (master-only to set) additionally unlocks settings, branding, rules, account-disable, and email changes.
users.searchTitlePivot: Clear the IP pivot to search by name again
users.searchTitle: Search username, email, or character name
users.loginTitle: Never = registered but no login session ever; active = has at least one login.
users.noFilterMatches: No users match the current filters.
users.colAgeTitle: Age bracket derived from the stored birth date. legacy = registered before birth dates were collected (attested 18+ at signup, counts as adult). Hover a cell for the exact date.
users.colIpsTitle: Up to 5 most-recent distinct IPs the user has been seen on. Captured on activity (login, connect, room switch, chat, posts), so this stays current as a user roams networks instead of showing only their original login IP. Click an IP to pivot the list to every account that shares it. Numeric badge = count of OTHER accounts seen on the same IP, flag for ban evasion or shared-device review.
users.ipChipTitle: Last seen {{time}}. Click to show every other account that has used this IP.
users.altBadgeTitle_one: {{count}} other account seen on this IP
users.altBadgeTitle_other: {{count}} other accounts seen on this IP
users.legacyTitle: No birth date on record. Registered before birth dates were collected, attested 18+ at signup, counts as an adult.
users.isolatedTitle: Isolation is on: this account only sees members under 18 and staff.
users.minorConfirm:
This makes "{{name}}" an under-18 account.

They will be signed out immediately, and 18+ rooms, profiles, topics, stories, and worlds will be hidden from their account. Continue?
users.noDobHint: No date on record (registered before birth dates were collected; counts as an adult). Leave empty to keep it that way.
users.dobHint: Corrections are audited. A date under 18 signs the user out and applies the age gates.
users.isolationLabel: Isolation (only sees members under 18 and staff)
users.isolationHint: Hides adult members from them and hides them from adults, both ways. Staff stay visible. Turns off by itself at 18.
users.lockedNote: This user is an owner. Only another owner can edit their profile or change their role.
users.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 chars.
users.resetPasswordHelp: The user's active sessions are dropped on reset; they'll have to log in again with the new password. Generate fills both fields with a 20-char password and copies it to the clipboard so you can paste it into whatever channel you're using to hand it back.
users.grantAmountError: Enter an XP or Currency amount (positive to grant, negative to revoke).
users.grantHelp: Goes through the live earning engine, rank recomputes, the user's wallet updates live, the ledger gets an audit row. Negative values revoke.
users.cosmeticsHelp: Grants bypass normal Currency / rank gates. Revokes also clear the equipped slot if the item was active. Both are idempotent.
users.resetEarningConfirm:
Hard-reset {{name}}'s earning state?

This wipes:
  • Master XP, Currency, rank, tier, and peak
  • Every character pool (XP / Currency / rank)
  • Owned name styles
  • Owned rank borders
  • Equipped cosmetics

Cannot be undone. Use for testing earning flows from scratch.
users.resetEarningHelp: Destructive test affordance, clears the user's XP / Currency / rank / peak, drops every character pool to zero, and removes all owned styles + borders. Useful for testing earning flows from a clean slate.

settings.retentionError: retention must be a duration like 30d (or 0 for never)
settings.ttlError: session TTL must be at least 5m
settings.idleGraceMinError: idle grace must be at least 30s
settings.idleGraceMaxError: idle grace must be 24h or less
settings.editGraceError: edit window must be a duration like 5m (or 0 to disable edits)
settings.editGraceMaxError: edit window must be 7 days or less
settings.description: Sitewide configuration. Changes apply immediately for new sessions and the next hourly retention sweep.
settings.retentionHelp: Messages older than this are purged hourly. <1>0</1> retains forever.
settings.idleTimeoutHelp: How long a user can be idle before they get bounced back to the login splash. Sliding: any keypress, mousemove, message, or room switch resets the clock. Min 5m.
settings.idleGhostHelp: When someone closes their tab or refreshes, they stay in the userlist faded out as "(idle)" for this long instead of vanishing. Inside the window, no connect/disconnect chat lines fire. The room they were in is also held open against archival. Min 30s, max 24h.
settings.maxEmailHint: 1 = traditional. Raise to allow shared/family accounts.
settings.maxRoomsHint: Cap on user-created rooms a single user may own. 0 disables user-created rooms.
settings.maxMsgLenHint: Hard cap on flat-chat body length (chars).
settings.maxDmLenHint: Hard cap on DM body length (chars). Independent from chat so private long-form conversations can have more room.
settings.maxForumLenHint: Hard cap on forum topic + reply body length (chars) in nested rooms. Typically larger than chat to allow long-form posts.
settings.maxForumTitleLenHint: Hard cap on a forum topic title (chars). Kept short so titles stay list-renderable in the topic picker.
settings.forumTopicsPerPageHint: How many non-sticky topics appear on each page of a forum category's numbered pagination strip. Stickies stay on page 1 only and don't count against this. Default 20.
settings.editWindowHint: How long after sending an author can edit / delete their own chat or DM message. Duration like 30s / 5m / 1h. 0 disables author edits entirely. Mods + admins always bypass this; forum posts are exempt and stay editable indefinitely.
settings.maxBioLenHint: Hard cap on profile bio HTML (chars).
settings.registrationHint: When closed, /auth/register returns 503 and the login screen hides the Register tab.
settings.activityFeedsHint: When off, the splash hides the "X users online" / room counters so an empty community doesn't telegraph "dead site" to first visitors. Flip on once there's a real pulse to surface.
settings.messages24hOn: On - splash shows rolling 24h message count
settings.messages24hOff: Off - splash hides the 24h message stat
settings.messages24hHint: Surfaces a rolling 24h chat-message count on the splash. Independent of Activity feeds, flip it on alone to show the message volume by itself, or pair with Activity feeds so it sits in the same row as the online/registered/room counters.
settings.featuredWorldsHint: Splash page picks up to 10 random open worlds and rotates them as a "settings you can play in" strip. Off by default; the seeded defaults plus any community open worlds will fill the rotation once enabled.
settings.designerOn: On - bio tab offers a visual Designer (desktop)
settings.designerOff: Off - bio editor is raw HTML source only
settings.designerHint: Adds a visual drag-and-drop Designer alongside the raw-HTML Source on the profile bio tab (desktop only). Off by default. Try it on your own profile before enabling site-wide; the Source view remains available either way.
settings.multiServerOn: On - server rail + join/create your own servers
settings.multiServerHint: Master switch for the multi-server feature: the round server-icon rail beside the userlist, the discover/create-a-server flow, and all per-server scoping. Off keeps the chat exactly as a single server. The SERVERS_KILL env var overrides this to off.
settings.antiSpamOn: On - rapid-fire floods get warned, then auto-muted
settings.antiSpamHint: Catches rapid-fire message floods: after more than five messages in a few seconds a user gets a warning and a short, growing cooldown, then an automatic mute (5 minutes, growing on repeat offenders) after three warnings. Keeps a room from being blown out when no mod is around. Trusted users, mods, and admins are exempt via the bypass_anti_spam permission.
settings.automodOn: On - messages are checked against your rules below
settings.automodHint: Master switch for content auto-moderation. When on, each message is checked against the rules below before it posts; a matching rule can warn (block the message), delete (silently drop it), or mute the sender. Off by default so mature roleplay isn't caught by surprise. Trusted users, mods, and admins are exempt via the bypass_automod permission. Manage rules and try sample text in the Auto-moderation section below.
settings.signupAgeOn: Allow ages 13 to 17 to sign up
settings.signupAgeOff: New accounts must be 18 or older
settings.signupAgeHint: When off, new accounts must be 18 or older. Existing accounts are not affected. This switch changes only the sign-up minimum; the 18+ content gates for under-18 accounts are always on.
settings.minorFilterHelp: Stars out strong language for members under 18, so a swear shows as d*** on their screen. A built-in list of hundreds of English swear words, including disguised spellings, is already active whenever this is on. The boxes below are only for extras and exceptions. Adults always see the message exactly as written, and the message itself is never changed.
settings.minorFilterOn: On - swearing is starred out for under-18 accounts
settings.minorFilterOff: Off - everyone sees messages exactly as written
settings.minorFilterTermsHint: Extra words to hide, one per line. The built-in list only knows English swearing - add Spanish or other-language words here.
settings.minorFilterAllowHint: Words the filter should always leave alone, one per line. Use this when an innocent word is getting starred out by mistake.
settings.defaultThemeHelp: Used when a user has no custom theme and no active character with a theme. Cleared (no override) means the built-in Parchment palette.
settings.noSiteDefault: No site default - using built-in Parchment.
settings.themeStyleHelp: The fallback visual treatment (ornaments, borders, textures) for palettes that don't have a design pinned below. Orthogonal to the palette, picking a style doesn't change which colors are used, just how they're rendered. Users can override this on their master or character profile.
settings.designsByThemeHelp: Pin a design to each named palette. When a user (or character) picks one of these themes, they get the paired design unless they've explicitly overridden it on their profile. "Use site default" means that theme falls through to the Theme style above. Custom palettes (anything not matching one of these presets) always fall through.
settings.muteLengthError: Mute length must be at least 1 minute (or blank for the default).
settings.automodRulesHelp: Rules checked against each message when Auto-moderation is on (toggle above). A rule can warn (block the message with a notice), delete it silently, or mute the sender. Trusted users, mods, and admins are always exempt. Off by default so mature roleplay isn't caught by surprise, use the test box below to blunt false positives before enabling.
settings.noRules: No rules yet. Add one below.
settings.linkNote: Matches any http/https link in the message.
settings.testHelp: Paste sample text to see which enabled rules would fire (and the resulting action). Nothing is saved or posted.
settings.testPlaceholder: Type or paste a message to test...
settings.noRulesFired: No rules fired, this message would post.
settings.minorFilterTryPh: Type a sentence to see what under-18 members would see
settings.minorFilterTryClean: No changes. That sentence shows exactly as written.

affiliates.title: Top RP Communities
affiliates.description: Partner community cards for the splash, ranked by traffic (busiest first). Members submit their own entries here for review; approved cards go live in the Top RP Communities section and each one carries a link-back the partner puts on their site.
affiliates.noCards: No cards yet. Add one, or approve a member submission to surface it in the Top RP Communities section.
affiliates.deleteCardConfirm: Delete this card? Its click stats and link-back go with it.
affiliates.legacyDescription: Old raw-HTML entries from topsite networks (toprpsites etc.). These render as verbatim badges, not cards, and are NOT sanitized so their tracking pixels pass through. Only paste HTML you trust. New partners should use cards instead.
affiliates.rejectPlaceholder: What needs changing? (shown to the submitter)
affiliates.linkBackLabel: Link-back (partner puts this on their site)
affiliates.resetPaddingTitle: Zero out the accumulated synthetic traffic
affiliates.descriptionPlaceholder: A short blurb shown on the card.
affiliates.paddingDescription: Adds fake daily visits, a random amount up to the max each day, spread out through the day, so a quiet community still shows some traffic. Real visit counts are never changed.
affiliates.htmlSnippetHelp: Paste verbatim from the affiliate's provided code. Tracking pixels and similar pass through unchanged.

announcements.scopeNote: These are the <1>System Announcements</1> for the default Spire server. Community servers manage their own announcements separately, so anything you add here shows to everyone on the home server (and site-wide chrome), not inside other communities.
announcements.bannerDescription: Rows shown here render in a rotating banner above the chat on the default Spire server. The marquee fades between entries every ~8 seconds when there are two or more enabled. Viewers can dismiss the bar for themselves; the dismissal persists in their browser until they clear site data.
announcements.scheduledDescription: Rows here fire as an every-room or single-room <1>/announce</1> on the default Spire server at the configured time. One-shot rows auto-disable after firing; recurring rows re-arm to <3>now + interval</3> on each fire (a server restart mid-cycle won't double-fire to catch up).
announcements.deleteBannerConfirm: Delete this banner? Viewers will lose it on next refresh.
announcements.bannerBodyPlaceholder: **Welcome** to the chat! Read the [house rules](/rules) before posting.
announcements.bodyCounter: {{length}} / {{max}}. Both formats supported; Markdown converts to HTML on save.
announcements.fireRoomConfirm: Fire this announcement now in the target room? (Does not change the schedule.)
announcements.fireAllConfirm: Fire this announcement now in EVERY room? (Does not change the schedule.)
announcements.fireNowTitle: Broadcast this announcement now. The schedule stays unchanged.
announcements.colorError: Color must be `#rrggbb` or a `theme:<slot>` token.
announcements.scheduledBodyPlaceholder: **Daily reminder:** Check the [event board](/rules#events) for tonight's sessions.
announcements.scheduleHelp: Interval (e.g. <1>1d8h</1>) for recurring, or an ISO datetime for a one-shot. Minimum interval is 1 minute; maximum 30 days.
announcements.chatPreview: Preview (as it will render in chat)

backups.busyDetailed: Another backup operation is in progress ({{kind}}: {{message}}). Wait for it to finish before starting another.
backups.busyGeneric: Server is busy with another backup operation. Try again in a moment.
backups.deleteSnapshotConfirm: Delete snapshot "{{filename}}"? This can't be undone.
backups.title: Backups
backups.description: Snapshot the database (full file) or the admin-customizable content (items, name styles, ranks, border frames, custom commands, settings, system rooms + worlds). Every snapshot is a ZIP that bundles the database payload AND every uploaded image (emoticon sheets, logos, rank sigil + border PNGs), so restoring on a fresh host comes up with everything intact. Each destructive import auto-saves a pre-restore safety snapshot so undo is one click away.
backups.busyOtherOp: Another backup operation is in progress ({{kind}}). Wait for it to finish.
backups.cannotImportMissingMigrations: Cannot import, this install is missing {{count}} migration(s) the backup expects. Deploy first.
backups.fullImportConfirm: Replacing the live database AND the uploads tree with this backup will sign every user out, drop unsaved changes, and restart the server. A pre-restore safety snapshot will be taken automatically. Continue?
backups.importingFull: Importing full archive, the server will restart…
backups.fullSubtitle: .zip, database.sqlite + uploads/ (everything: users, messages, earning, cosmetics, all images)
backups.invalidArchive: File failed validation, not a valid backup archive, or the inner database isn't from a Spire install (missing the <1>users.system</1> sentinel row that every Spire install seeds).
backups.targetMissingMigrations: ⚠ Target install is missing {{count}} migration(s) the backup expects. Deploy before importing:
backups.extraMigrationsNote: Note: this install has {{count}} migration(s) ahead of the backup. Import will still work; newer columns get default values.
backups.fullImportButton: Import (replaces live DB + uploads, restarts server)
backups.mirrorConfirm: MIRROR RESTORE: this will DELETE {{wipe}} row(s) currently on this install and REPLACE them with {{add}} row(s) from the backup, across {{tables}} tables. It will also REPLACE the live uploads tree with the {{files}} file(s) bundled in the archive. A pre-import safety snapshot will be saved automatically so you can roll back if needed. Continue?
backups.contentImportDone: Imported: {{inserted}} row(s) added, {{files}} upload file(s) restored. Pre-import snapshot: {{id}}
backups.contentSubtitle: .zip, content.json (every exportable table) + uploads/ (every uploaded image)
backups.installMissingMigrations: ⚠ This install is missing {{count}} migration(s) the backup expects:
backups.bundledUploadsReplace: Bundled uploads: <1>{{count}}</1> file(s), <3>{{bytes}}</3>, will replace the live <5>/uploads/</5> tree on import.
backups.mirrorExplainer: Content backup is a <1>mirror restore</1>, every table the document carries gets wiped on this install and replaced with the source rows, and the entire <3>/uploads/</3> tree is replaced with the archive's. Tables not in the document are left untouched. A pre-import safety snapshot is saved automatically so you can roll back.
backups.snapshotsHelp: Manual snapshots are downloads you triggered. Pre-import snapshots are taken automatically before each restore so you can undo. Up to 10 of each kind are retained; the oldest are pruned automatically.

branding.logoColorError: Logo color must be a 6-digit hex like #2c5d2c (or empty to clear).
branding.description: Public branding shown to every user (including the login screen). Changes apply immediately for everyone after save.
branding.siteNameHelp: Shown in the banner, login screen, BootSplash, and tab title. Empty falls back to <1>The Spire</1>.
branding.siteUrlHelp: When set, the banner wraps the site name / logo image in an unstyled link pointing here, useful for sending visitors back to a marketing landing page or the main domain when the chat lives at a subdomain. The wrapping is invisible (no underline, no color change); the logo still reads as a logo, it just becomes clickable. Must start with <1>http://</1> or <3>https://</3>; leave empty to disable.
branding.bannerCoverHelp: Full CSS <1>background</1> shorthand applied behind the logo text. Accepts <3>url()</3>, <5>linear-gradient(...)</5>, a solid color, etc. Leave empty to use the theme's panel color.
branding.logoImageHelp: Shown in the banner and on the splash in place of the site name. Leave empty to use the text title instead. The default install ships <1>/thespire-logo.png</1>; uploading replaces it with your own image stored on the server (no external host required).
branding.logoColorClearTitle: Clear - logo follows the active theme.
branding.logoFontHelp: A CSS <1>font-family</1> stack. Web fonts must be self-hosted or loaded via <3>@import</3> in your stylesheet - this field only changes the family name, not the loading. Empty to use the built-in serif stack.
branding.welcomePlaceholder:
Welcome to The Spire - a roleplay-focused chat sanctuary.

Sign in to enter, or register a new account.
branding.welcomeHelp: HTML rendered above the splash login/register form. Sanitized server-side using the same allow-list as profile bios - basic formatting tags, links, lists, and headings (h3-h6) are accepted. Empty hides the welcome block entirely.
branding.seoDescPlaceholder: A roleplay-focused chat sanctuary. Build characters, share scenes, and tell collaborative stories with other writers.
branding.seoDescHelp: Plain-text description used in <1><meta name="description"></1>and the OG / Twitter card. Search engines typically display the first ~155 characters. Empty falls back to the welcome message stripped to text.
branding.ogImageHelp: The image shown when someone shares a link on Discord, X, Slack, or Facebook. Used as the default for every page that doesn't have its own card (a shared forum or community uses its own banner). Recommended 1200x630, under 1 MB. Empty falls back to the built-in card.
branding.taglineHelp: Added after the site name in the browser tab and search results on the homepage, login, and register pages, so the title reads <1>{{title}}</1>. Keeps the title keyword-rich without baking search terms into the brand name itself. Empty falls back to <3>{{fallback}}</3>.
branding.seoKeywordsPlaceholder: roleplay chat, RP chat, play-by-post forum, community hosting, worldbuilding
branding.seoKeywordsHelp: Comma-separated keyword list for <1><meta name="keywords"></1>. Google ignores this, but Bing, DuckDuckGo, and some card scrapers still read it. Empty falls back to the built-in default list.
branding.verificationHelp: Paste only the <1>content</1> token from the verification tag, not the whole <3><meta></3>. Each gets added to the page <5><head></5> when set. Leave empty once you've switched to the DNS or file verification method.
branding.socialProfilesHelp: One profile URL per line. Added to the structured data so search engines can connect these accounts to your site. Non-link lines are ignored.
branding.indexingAllow: Allow search engines to index this site
branding.indexingOnHelp: Search engines may crawl and index the site normally. Turn this off for a staging or pre-launch install you don't want appearing in search yet.
branding.indexingOffHelp: <0>Off:</0> the site tells all search engines not to index it, and over time it will be removed from Google and other search results. Only use this before launch.
branding.serpHelp: How the homepage looks in a Google result and a Discord / X link card. Live from the values above.
branding.customHeadPlaceholder:
<!-- Plausible -->
<script defer data-domain="example.com" src="https://plausible.io/js/script.js"></script>

<!-- or Google Analytics, Cloudflare Web Analytics, Umami, etc. -->
branding.customHeadHelp: <0>Raw HTML, not sanitized.</0> Pasted verbatim into <2><head></2>on every splash response so analytics fire before React mounts. Anything you put here ships to every visitor on first paint - double-check the snippet from your provider's dashboard before saving.
branding.logoTypeError: Only PNG, JPEG, WebP, and GIF are accepted.
branding.logoSizeError: Image is over 8MB. Resize or recompress before uploading.
branding.logoClearTitle: Clear, banner falls back to the text site name.

review.declinePrompt: Decline "{{name}}"? Optional note shown to the applicant (they can re-apply after the cooldown):
review.queueClear: No applications waiting. The queue is clear.
review.readOnlyQueue: You can view the queue; deciding requires the review permission.

forums.title: Forum applications
forums.description: Approving creates the forum with the applicant as owner, a starter board, and a welcome topic. Declining starts the re-apply cooldown; the note is shown to the applicant.
forums.approveConfirm:
Approve "{{name}}" (/f/{{slug}})?

{{applicant}} becomes its owner; the forum + a starter board are created immediately.
forums.archiveConfirm: Archive "{{name}}"? It leaves the catalog (boards and topics are kept) and can be restored here.

servers.title: Server applications
servers.description: Approving creates the server with the applicant as owner, a starter room, and a welcome message. Declining starts the re-apply cooldown; the note is shown to the applicant.
servers.approveConfirm:
Approve "{{name}}" (/s/{{slug}})?

{{applicant}} becomes its owner; the server + a starter room are created immediately.
servers.archiveConfirm: Archive "{{name}}"? It leaves the catalog (rooms and messages are kept) and can be restored here.
servers.suspendPrompt: Suspend "{{name}}"? It's put under review and blocked to everyone but its owner, that server's staff, and global staff (who can enter to fix it). Optional note shown to blocked users:
servers.liftBanConfirm: Lift the ban on "{{name}}"? It becomes fully accessible again.
servers.liftSuspensionConfirm: Lift the suspension on "{{name}}"? It becomes fully accessible again.
servers.enterTitle: Enter this server's chat to moderate
servers.liftTitle: Lift the suspension / ban — the server becomes accessible again
servers.suspendTitle: Put under review — block everyone but the owner and staff
servers.banTitle: Ban for a set duration — auto-lifts when it expires
servers.deleteTitleAttr: Permanently delete this server and all its data
servers.banDescription: Blocks everyone but the owner, that server's staff, and global staff. Auto-lifts when the duration elapses.
servers.banReasonPlaceholder: Optional note shown to blocked users beneath the ban notice.
servers.deleteModalBody: This permanently removes the server, its rooms and messages, members, invites, and every scrap of its data. There is no undo.

reports.title: Reports queue
reports.reviewPrompt: Mark report as reviewed (acted on). Optional note for the audit log:
reports.dismissPrompt: Dismiss report (no action). Optional note for the audit log:

analytics.noData: No data in this range yet.
analytics.saveFailedPermission: Save failed (needs the Edit site settings permission).
analytics.geoReverted: Reverted to the bundled country database.
analytics.geoSavedActive: Saved. GeoLite2-City database downloaded and active.
analytics.geoAccuracyHint: Country geo works out of the box from a bundled database. For fresher, city-level accuracy, paste a free MaxMind Account ID + License key (from your MaxMind account). The key is stored securely and never shown again.
analytics.geoKeySavedError: Key saved, but the database isn't loaded yet: {{error}}
analytics.geoKeySaved: Key saved, but the database isn't loaded yet.
analytics.geoHint: Coarse country from IP (bundled GeoLite2). Older hits recorded before this was enabled show no country. Edge Fly-Region is stored as a weak fallback tag on each hit.
analytics.inAppHint: Where authed users go inside the app: modals opened, admin/server sub-tabs switched to, rooms and servers entered, and other tracked features.
analytics.noPublicPages: No public page views recorded yet.
analytics.entityHint: Which specific public pages got viewed, broken out by name instead of collapsed into the path template above. Deleted entities show their old slug marked (deleted).

emoticons.title: Emoticon sheets
emoticons.description: Each sheet is a 4×4 grid. The bottom row is reserved for future cells, leave any label blank or "empty" to hide a cell from the picker.
emoticons.imageLabel: Sheet image (PNG / JPG / WebP / GIF, 4×4 grid)
emoticons.deleteConfirm: Delete "{{name}}" ({{slug}})? Every reaction placed with this sheet on any chat message, DM, or forum post will also be removed. Cannot be undone.
emoticons.cellLabelsHelp: Cell labels (4×4, row-major). Empty or "empty" = hidden from picker.
emoticons.rejectPrompt:
Reject "{{name}}" from {{submitter}}?

Optional reason (shown to the submitter):
emoticons.rejectedAlert: Rejected. Refunded {{amount}} Currency to the submitter.
emoticons.submissionsDescription: Reject refunds the paid Currency and deletes the image. Approve makes the sheet live for everyone.

modCases.title: Moderation case log
modCases.description: A record of complaints, reports, disputes, and notes handled by staff: who complained, about whom or what, and how it was resolved. Reporter and subject accept a plain name or an <1>@id:</1>/<3>@cid:</3> token to link the exact identity. This is separate from the user-filed Reports queue.
modCases.statusSelectTitle: Optionally change status with this update
modCases.deleteNoteConfirm: Delete this note? This cannot be undone (its updates + backed-up evidence go too).
modCases.deleteCaseConfirm: Delete this case? This cannot be undone (its updates + backed-up evidence go too).
modCases.identityGone: That identity no longer exists (renamed away or deleted).
modCases.backupPartial: Backed up {{found}} of {{requested}} messages ({{missing}} not found, already purged?).
modCases.resolutionPlaceholder: Leave blank while open; add progress via Updates after saving
modCases.evidenceHelp: Snapshots those chat messages onto the case so they survive cleanup. Add more anytime by editing.

panel.title: Admin
panel.homeServerScope: Manages the home server. Each community server keeps its own in its server console.

tabDesc.overview: Your site at a glance: who is online, new sign-ups, and how busy chat has been.
tabDesc.analytics: See where visitors come from and which parts of the site people actually use.
tabDesc.audit: A running record of every staff action, so you can always check who did what.
tabDesc.reports: Complaints members have filed. Look at each one and decide what happens.
tabDesc.mod-cases: Your moderation casebook: write up incidents, add updates, and save evidence.
tabDesc.verify-logs: Paste an exported chat log to check it is real and has not been edited.
tabDesc.permissions: Decide what each role is allowed to do, or fine-tune one person's powers.
tabDesc.users: Look up any account to change its role, help with a password, or handle trouble.
tabDesc.scriptorium: Reports about stories in the Scriptorium. Review them and act if needed.
tabDesc.forums: Approve or decline requests for new forums, and manage the ones already open.
tabDesc.servers: Approve or decline requests for new servers, and step into any of them.
tabDesc.email: Send mail to members: a single person, everyone, or a newsletter.
tabDesc.settings: The main switchboard: sign-ups, limits, safety filters, and site defaults.
tabDesc.branding: Your site's name, logo, welcome text, and how it shows up in search results.
tabDesc.rules: Write the rules and the notices people agree to when they sign up.
tabDesc.links: Add your own links to the top banner, like a wiki or a partner site.
tabDesc.affiliates: The partner cards in the Top RP Communities section, and member submissions.
tabDesc.system: Live server health, plus restart and cleanup tools for emergencies.
tabDesc.backups: Save a full copy of everything, and restore one if something goes wrong.

search.noResults: Nothing found. Try a different word.

redirect.languageHint: Not in this panel. Open the Menu and look under Display.
redirect.profile: Your name, avatar, and profile style
redirect.profileHint: Not in this panel. Open Edit Profile from the menu.
redirect.notificationsHint: Not in this panel. Open Edit Profile from the menu.
redirect.serverAdminHint: Each server has its own admin. Open it from the server's gear icon.

email.cancelConfirm: Cancel this campaign? Already-sent emails can't be recalled; the rest won't go out.
email.broadcastConfirm: Send this to everyone subscribed now? It will go out in daily batches.
email.newsletterConfirmScheduled: Send this newsletter to everyone who hasn't unsubscribed from Newsletter (scheduled for {{when}})? It goes out in daily batches.
email.newsletterConfirmNow: Send this newsletter to everyone who hasn't unsubscribed from Newsletter (sent now)? It goes out in daily batches.
email.notConfigured: Email isn't configured yet. Set the <1>BREVO_API_KEY</1> secret. Sending will be skipped until it's set.
email.broadcastInfo: Goes to every account with an email that hasn't unsubscribed from <1>{{label}}</1> (everyone is included by default), in daily batches of up to {{cap}}. The footer link lets a recipient unsubscribe from this category only.
email.newsletterInfo: Goes to every account with an email that hasn't unsubscribed from <1>Newsletter</1> (everyone is included by default). Pick a delivery time (or leave it blank to send now); it goes out in daily batches starting then, and skips anyone who unsubscribes before it sends.
email.deliverAt: Deliver at (leave blank to send now)
email.requireVerification: Require new users to verify their email after registering.
email.modeNudge: Nudge: account works, dismissible reminder banner
email.verificationNote: Existing accounts are treated as already verified, so turning this on only affects new sign-ups.

overview.description: Live snapshot of activity across the site. Auto-refreshes every 30 seconds.
overview.registeredUsersHint: Total accounts, excluding the system sentinel.
overview.activeUsersHint: Distinct logins inside each window. Older buckets undercount if session TTL is shorter than the window.
overview.chatMessagesHint: Chat volume, excludes presence/system rows and soft-deleted messages.
overview.forumActivityHint: Topics and replies across all nested-mode rooms.
overview.moderation7dHint: Reports filed and audit-log actions in the last week.
overview.noNewAccounts: No new accounts in the last 5 days.

rules.description: The app-wide governing rules and the privacy notice shown when users click the Rules button. The rules here apply everywhere and show on every server and on the site. Each server can also post its own Server Rules in Server Admin, Settings. Both fields accept the same HTML allow-list as profile bios: formatting tags, links, lists, and headings (h3 to h6).
rules.appRulesHelp: The app-wide governing rules. They apply on every server and on the site, and always appear under the App Rules tab. A server's own Server Rules are written separately in Server Admin, Settings, and sit beside these in their own tab. Defaults seed an 8-point baseline covering consent, godmodding, OOC and IC separation, and reporting. Cap is 1MB of HTML, enough for a fully comprehensive multi-section ruleset.
rules.securityHelp: Shown alongside the rules. Defaults explain the privacy contract: admins cannot read private/whispered messages, so users should self-govern and report problems with screenshots. 500KB cap fits a full privacy disclosure with ample headroom.
rules.disclaimerHelp: Rendered above the registration form on the splash. Users must tick an "I agree" checkbox before <1>/auth/register</1> succeeds. Empty disclaimer = no checkbox shown (registration unblocked). 500KB cap fits a full Terms-of-Service document.
rules.serverRegHelp: Shown with an "I agree" checkbox when someone applies to register a server. Empty = no agreement gate shown.
rules.forumRegHelp: Shown with an "I agree" checkbox when someone applies to create a forum. Empty = no agreement gate shown.
rules.welcomeHelp: Onboarding modal shown once to users who register <1>after</1> the most recent save here. Existing users (registered before the welcome was set or last edited) never see it - this is for fresh accounts only, not a broadcast channel. Re-saving with the same text doesn't re-shift the audience cutoff; only changing the text does. Empty text = no welcome shown.
rules.previewAgree: I have read and accept the disclaimer above and the house rules.
rules.previewWelcomeCaption: new-user welcome modal (shown only to accounts registered after this is saved)
rules.previewNote: Preview is run through DOMPurify, but tags outside the server's allow-list will still disappear on save (server uses sanitize-html with a stricter list than DOMPurify's default).

scriptorium.title: Scriptorium reports
scriptorium.noMatches: No reports match the current filters.
scriptorium.forceRatePrompt: Force-rate the parent story. Enter a rating ({{ratings}})
scriptorium.auditNotePrompt: Optional note for the audit log
scriptorium.revokeConfirm: Also revoke the author's XP/currency earned from this book? (use for blatant rule-breaking / economy farming)
scriptorium.deleteConfirm: HARD DELETE "{{title}}"? This cascades to chapters, reviews, and reports. Cannot be undone.

system.maintenanceDescription: Destructive live-ops actions. Each one is logged to the Audit tab. Use with care.
system.restartDescription: Drops live connections for a few seconds, then the app reloads.
system.purgeDescription: Permanently deletes every chat message site-wide. Irreversible. Rooms and accounts are untouched. Type <1>PURGE</1> to enable.

links.description: Banner links shown to all users. The Exit/logout link is built-in.
links.empty: No links yet. Add one above.

verifyLog.emptyError: Paste a log or drop the exported .html file first.
verifyLog.verdictAltered: Altered or not from this server
verifyLog.verdictAuthentic: Authentic: signature valid, matches server record
verifyLog.verdictMismatch: Signature valid but does NOT match the server's recorded export
verifyLog.verdictMismatchDetail: The receipt exists but its content hash differs. Treat with suspicion.
verifyLog.verdictNoReceipt: Signature valid: no server receipt on file
verifyLog.verdictNoReceiptDetail: The signature checks out, but no matching receipt was found (it may have predated receipts or been pruned).
verifyLog.description: Paste a submitted chat log, or drop the exported <1>.html</1> file. The log is checked against the server: a green result means it is authentic and unaltered. Editing any part of a log breaks the check.
verifyLog.dropPlaceholder: Drop the exported log here, or paste its contents…
